mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a contraction in the industrial production index signals supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 5-day returns, or the signal loses predictive power when filtered by cycle phase.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0 percent indicates restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations and corporate investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5 on the resulting trading strategy.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which propagate through supply chains to boost corporate earnings and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases do not correlate with positive 60-day returns, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance levels in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, an industrial production index above 105 signals rapid capacity utilization and inventory restocking, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels above 105 during recovery phases fail to precede positive 5-day returns, or the mechanism shows no statistical significance in regime-filtered backtests.
